Landing.title: Manage Clients
Landing.subtitle: More Efficiently & Professionally
Landing.description: A client management system for bookings, invoices, settlements, and follow-ups. Built for freelance vendors, creative studios, and service businesses that want a cleaner, more professional workflow.
Landing.trustedBy: Trusted by vendors & freelancers in Indonesia
Landing.problemSubtitle: Do you experience these issues every day?
Landing.problem1Desc: Booking information spread across WhatsApp, notes, and different spreadsheets. Hard to track.
Landing.problem2Desc: Creating invoices one by one, calculating remaining balances, and tracking down payments manually.
Landing.problem3Desc: No centralized system to check schedules, leading to frequent double bookings.
Landing.problem4Desc: Not knowing which bookings are completed, unpaid, or still in the queue.
Landing.problem5Desc: Retyping booking confirmation messages to each client takes up your valuable time.
Landing.problem6Desc: No clear revenue summary, mixed-up down payments, and difficult to create financial reports.
Landing.featuresSubtitle: Manage bookings, invoices, client tracking, and vendor operations in one cleaner system.
Landing.feature1Desc: Manage bookings, session schedules, and client operations from one organized dashboard.
Landing.feature2Desc: Create invoices, monitor deposits, open final settlements, and follow up on payments without manual spreadsheets.
Landing.feature3Desc: Send booking confirmations, session reminders, invoices, and client follow-ups through ready-to-use WhatsApp templates.
Landing.feature4Desc: Track booking progress and share a tracking link so clients can check updates on their own.
Landing.feature5Desc: Sync booking schedules to Google Calendar so your team schedule always stays up to date.
Landing.feature6Desc: Share an online booking form so client data flows straight into your dashboard.
Landing.workflowSubtitle: Three simple steps to start managing your clients
Landing.step1Desc: Create a free account, set up your vendor profile, and add your services/packages.
Landing.step2Desc: Clients fill in the online booking form, or you add them manually from the dashboard.
Landing.step3Desc: Manage schedules, send WhatsApp templates, monitor client tracking, and handle invoicing through settlement.
Landing.pricingSubtitle: Starting from Rp 39,000/month. Small investment, big results for your business. Try free for 5 days!
Landing.faqSectionSubtitle: Answers to common questions about Client Desk
Landing.ctaTitle: Ready to Manage Clients More Professionally?
Landing.ctaSubtitle: Join now and experience the ease of managing bookings, invoices, and client schedules.
Landing.ctaTrialNote: No credit card needed. 5-day trial, then choose the right plan for you.

FAQ.pageDescription: Find answers to common questions about Client Desk here.
FAQ.a1: Client Desk is a client management platform for freelance vendors, creative studios, and service businesses. Manage bookings, invoices, schedules, client tracking, and WhatsApp communication in one dashboard.
FAQ.a2: Client Desk offers a free trial so you can try all features. After the trial, you can choose a subscription plan starting from Rp 39,000/month.
FAQ.a3: Client Desk is ideal for freelance vendors, photographers, videographers, wedding organizers, creative studios, and other service businesses that need a clean and professional client management system.
FAQ.a4: Yes, we use encryption and secure cloud infrastructure to protect your data. Data is stored on encrypted servers protected with industry-standard security measures.
FAQ.a5: Simply register an account, set up your vendor profile, add your services/packages, and you're ready to receive bookings from clients.
FAQ.q6: How much does Client Desk cost?
FAQ.a6: Client Desk is available starting from Rp 39,000/month, Rp 99,000/3 months, or Rp 349,000/year.
FAQ.a7: We accept payment via bank transfer, e-wallets (GoPay, OVO, Dana), and QRIS.
FAQ.a8: Yes, we offer a 7-day money-back guarantee after purchase if you feel Client Desk doesn't meet your needs.
FAQ.q9: What are the main features of Client Desk?
FAQ.a9: Key features include booking and session management, invoices, deposits and settlement, WhatsApp templates, client status tracking, Google Calendar sync, online booking forms, special booking links, Google Drive, freelancer assignment, Excel import, and custom domain branding.
FAQ.q10: Does Client Desk support multiple languages?
FAQ.a10: Yes, Client Desk supports Bahasa Indonesia and English. You can switch languages at any time.
FAQ.q11: Can I access it from my phone?
FAQ.a11: Yes, Client Desk is fully responsive and can be accessed from your phone, tablet, or desktop browser.
FAQ.q12: Do I need to install an app?
FAQ.a12: No, Client Desk is a web application that runs in your browser. Just open the website and log in, no installation needed.

Pricing.pageDescription: Affordable pricing for all vendors. Small investment, big results for your business.
Pricing.billingManualRenew: No auto-debit. Renewal is done manually.
Pricing.crossProductHint: Need an online client photo selection system?
Pricing.trialDescription: Enjoy a 5-day free trial to experience Client Desk's core workflows without limitations.

Features.pageDescription: Manage bookings, invoices, client tracking, and vendor operations in one modern platform.
Features.feature1Desc: Manage manual and form-based bookings, organize session details, and keep client operations in one dashboard.
Features.feature2Desc: Create professional invoices, monitor deposits, manage final settlements, and review payment status without messy manual work.
Features.feature3Desc: Use WhatsApp templates for booking confirmations, session reminders, invoices, settlements, and faster client follow-ups.
Features.feature4Desc: Track each booking from queue to completion, then share a tracking link so clients can follow progress themselves.
Features.feature5Desc: Sync booking schedules to Google Calendar so admin and team calendars stay accurate without re-entry.
Features.feature6Desc: Share an online booking form that feeds directly into your dashboard with custom fields, packages, and initial payment flow.
Features.feature7Desc: Create special booking links with package, add-on, discount, or deposit rules tailored to a specific offer.
Features.feature8Desc: Manage Google Drive work folders and control when photo or video delivery links become visible on the client tracking page.
Features.feature9Desc: Manage team members and freelancers, then assign them to bookings so field operations stay organized.
Features.feature10Desc: Import booking data from Excel or CSV in bulk to speed up migration and routine admin entry.
Features.feature11Desc: Build your service catalog with main packages, add-ons, pricing, and durations so offers stay consistent.
Features.feature12Desc: Strengthen your studio branding with a custom domain, SEO settings, and more professional client-facing links.

Terms.section1Content: By accessing and using Client Desk services, you agree to and are bound by these terms and conditions. If you do not agree with these terms, please do not use our services.
Terms.section2Content: Client Desk is a web-based client management platform for freelance vendors, creative studios, and service businesses. Services include booking management, invoicing, settlements, client tracking, WhatsApp integration, Google Calendar synchronization, online booking forms, and Google Drive storage.
Terms.section3Content: You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account and password. You agree to immediately notify us of any unauthorized use of your account. We are not responsible for any loss arising from unauthorized use of your account.
Terms.section4Content: Subscription fees are charged according to the plan you choose (monthly, quarterly, or yearly). Payments made are non-refundable, except within the 7-day guarantee period after the first purchase.
Terms.section5Content: You agree to use the service only for lawful purposes and in accordance with applicable laws. It is prohibited to use the service for illegal activities, spread malware, or disrupt other users' services.
Terms.section6Content: We respect your privacy and manage data in accordance with our Privacy Policy. Please read our Privacy Policy for more information about how we collect, use, and protect your data.
Terms.section7Content: Client Desk is provided "as is" without any warranties. We are not responsible for any indirect, incidental, or consequential damages arising from the use or inability to use the service.
Terms.section8Content: We reserve the right to modify these terms and conditions at any time. Changes will take effect once published on this page. Continued use of the service after changes constitutes your acceptance of the updated terms.
Terms.section9Content: If you have questions about these terms and conditions, please contact us via Instagram @ryanekoapps.

Privacy.section1Content: We collect information you provide when registering an account, including your name, email address, and profile information. We also collect usage data such as activity logs and setting preferences.
Privacy.section2Content: The information we collect is used to provide and improve Client Desk services, process payments, send service-related notifications, and protect user security.
Privacy.section3Content: Client Desk integrates with third-party services such as Google (Calendar, Drive) and WhatsApp. When you use these integrations, necessary data is shared in accordance with each service's respective privacy policies.
Privacy.section4Content: We use encryption and industry-standard security measures to protect your data. However, no method of electronic transmission or storage is completely secure.
Privacy.section5Content: Your data is retained for as long as your account is active or as needed to provide services. You can request account and data deletion at any time by contacting us.
Privacy.section6Content: You have the right to access, update, or delete your personal information. You also have the right to withdraw consent for data use at any time.
Privacy.section7Content: We use cookies to improve user experience, store preferences, and analyze service usage. You can configure your browser to reject cookies.
Privacy.section8Content: We may update this privacy policy from time to time. Significant changes will be notified via email or in-app notifications.
Privacy.section9Content: For privacy-related questions, contact us via Instagram @ryanekoapps.

promo.popupDesc: Boost your studio branding with a custom domain. Clients will trust you more seeing your own domain name.
promo.benefit1: More professional branding — clients see your studio name in the URL
promo.benefit3: Look more professional and trustworthy to clients
promo.oneTimeAppliesBoth: one-time payment, valid for Fastpik & Client Desk

page.heroDesc: Use your own domain to look more professional. Your clients will be more impressed and trusting.
page.brandDesc: Your own domain makes your studio look more established and trustworthy to clients.
page.urlDesc: From clientdesk.ryanekoapp.web.id/... to booking.yourstudio.com/... — much easier to remember!
page.seoDesc: Your own domain helps improve your studio's visibility on search engines.
page.customDesc: Logo, favicon, and appearance can be customized to match your studio brand.
page.separateDesc: Your studio has its own identity, separate from Client Desk's main domain.
page.proDesc: Clients will be more confident and impressed seeing your own studio domain name.
page.pricingInc1Desc: SSL, Nginx, DNS — all handled by us
page.pricingInc2Desc: Logo, favicon, and appearance matching your brand
page.pricingInc3Desc: Technical assistance if any issues arise
page.pricingDomainNote: Price does not include domain cost. Domain prices vary (~Rp 35k-300k/year depending on extension).
page.ctaDesc: Contact us via Instagram for consultation and custom domain setup.

SpecialBookingForm.lockedAddonValidation: When Add-on is locked, choose at least one add-on.
SpecialBookingForm.vendorSlugNotReady: Vendor slug is not available yet. Please check profile first.
SpecialBookingForm.pageSubtitle: Create special booking links with lock/unlock rules for Package and Add-on, plus accommodation fee and fixed discount.

SpecialBookingForm.packageHint: When unlocked, options below become optional prefill.

Track.notFoundDesc: Tracking link is invalid or booking has been deleted.
Track.name: Name
Track.settlementReadyBannerTitle: Final invoice and settlement form are ready
Track.settlementReadyBannerDescription: You can now open the final invoice and continue the settlement process for this booking.
Track.settlementReadyBannerHint: The current remaining settlement is {amount}. Payment status: {status}.
Track.settlementReadyBannerHintSubmitted: The settlement proof has been submitted. The vendor team will verify your payment first.
Track.settlementReadyBannerHintPaid: This booking has been fully paid. The final invoice is still available to open again from this section.
Track.fileReadyBannerDescription: The final files for this booking are now available to access.
Track.fileReadyBannerHint: Check the File Results section below to open them.
Track.videoGatewayDescription: The video result for {name} is ready to open.
Track.videoUnavailableDescription: The video link is not available yet or is not ready to be shown for this booking status.
Track.settlementNotOpened: Final settlement has not been opened by the admin yet. Please contact the vendor if you need the final invoice.

Settlement.title: Settlement Form
Settlement.noAdjustments: There are no final add-ons for this booking yet.
Settlement.notOpened: The admin has not opened final settlement for this booking yet. Please contact the vendor to request the final invoice.
Settlement.submittedInfo: Your settlement proof has been submitted. The vendor will verify your payment first.
Settlement.confirmAdminInstruction: Please continue by confirming to the admin via WhatsApp so verification can be processed faster.
Settlement.adminWhatsappNotAvailable: Admin WhatsApp number is not available yet. Please contact the vendor via other channels.
Settlement.paidInfo: This booking has already been marked as fully paid. Thank you.
Settlement.paymentFormDesc: Choose a payment method, complete the payment, then upload the proof for admin verification.
Settlement.bankEmpty: No active bank accounts are available.
Settlement.qrisLoadError: The QRIS image could not be loaded on this device. Try refreshing the page or contact the admin.
Settlement.paymentMethodBankDesc: Choose a bank account for payment
Settlement.paymentMethodCashDesc: Pay in cash to the admin

Settlement.paymentProofQrisHint: Upload payment proof after paying via QRIS.
Settlement.dragDropHint: Or drag and drop a file here (JPG, PNG, PDF max 5MB).

Settlement.defaultGreeting: Please review your booking details below, then complete the remaining payment using one of the available payment methods.
Settlement.errorMethod: Please choose a payment method first.
Settlement.errorSource: Please choose a bank account first.
Settlement.errorProof: Payment proof is required for bank transfer or QRIS.

BatchImport.dialogDescription: Paste data from a spreadsheet, validate first, then commit when errors = 0.
BatchImport.guidanceUniversity: University: use full name, display name (for example: Universitas Indonesia (UI)), or a unique abbreviation (for example: UI).
BatchImport.guidanceSessionDate: Session Date: supports DD/MM/YYYY, DD-MM-YYYY, YYYY-MM-DD, or Excel serial.
BatchImport.guidanceSessionTime: Session Time: supports HH:mm, HH.mm, or HHmm.
BatchImport.guidanceBookingDate: Booking Date: supports flexible date formats or Excel serial; when empty it falls back to session date (warning).
BatchImport.guidanceWisudaOnly: For Wisuda events, university must be selected from suggestions (not manual typing).
BatchImport.pasteTipsLabel: Tip: click one cell then paste a data block (Ctrl/Cmd + V)
BatchImport.uploadPrimaryHint: Paste data from Excel/Google Sheets into the input area
BatchImport.uploadHintAutoId: External import ID is auto-generated, no manual input needed
BatchImport.uploadHintAutoValidate: Click Validate after finishing your paste
BatchImport.commitLockedMessage: Commit is locked because errors still exist.

Onboarding.title: Set up Client Desk step by step
Onboarding.description: Complete these core steps so your dashboard, booking form, and main workflow are ready to use right away.
Onboarding.optionalDescription: Connect Google if you want calendar and file automations after the core setup is finished.
Onboarding.completeDescription: Your Client Desk workspace is ready to use. You can reopen this setup guide anytime.

reviewModal.title: Client Desk setup progress
reviewModal.description: Review the steps you've completed and reopen any guide you still want to follow.
reviewModal.progressDescription: You can continue from the next step or reopen a specific guide anytime.

Onboarding.fallbackMessage: The highlighted element is not available on this screen right now. Use this guide as a hint and continue when you're ready.

overlay.title: Let's finish the initial setup
overlay.description: To get Client Desk ready, complete these 8 core setup steps. We'll guide you to the most important area on each page.
overlay.dismissForever: Don't show this again on this device

profile.label: Complete Profile
profile.description: Fill in the basic account identity so your admin area and public pages look more polished.
profile.tourBody: Complete your basic account identity first. This name will be used across the admin area and a few public-facing views.
profile.tourAction: Enter the name you want to use here, then save the changes before moving on.

studioSettings.label: Set Studio Name
studioSettings.description: Fill in your studio name in General Settings so the booking form identity and link are ready to use properly.
studioSettings.tourTitle: Fill in your studio name first
studioSettings.tourBody: Fill in your studio name first in General Settings. This name is used for your booking form identity and link.
studioSettings.tourAction: Complete the studio name in this section, then save before moving on to the booking form.

services.label: Create Services or Packages
services.description: Add at least one service so the booking form and manual booking flow can work properly.
services.tourBody: Add at least one service or package so clients can use your booking form.
services.tourAction: Use this add button to create your first main package. This step will continue after the package is saved successfully.
services.blockingMessage: Save your first main package in this popup before continuing.

team.label: Add Team/Freelancer
team.description: Add at least one member so bookings can be assigned or managed collaboratively more cleanly.
team.tourBody: Add at least one team member or freelancer so booking assignments and collaborative work stay organized.
team.tourAction: Use this add button to create your first team member or freelancer. This step will continue after the member is saved successfully.
team.blockingMessage: Save your first team member or freelancer in this popup before continuing.

formBooking.label: Set Up Booking Form
formBooking.description: Prepare the booking form link and payment methods so clients can book on their own with a cleaner flow.
formBooking.tourBody: Set the booking form slug or link and payment methods so clients can book on their own more smoothly.
formBooking.tourAction: Choose at least one payment method, save the settings, then check the form link or preview if needed.

bookingFirst.label: Create First Booking
bookingFirst.description: Enter your first booking so you can see the Client Desk workflow end-to-end.
bookingFirst.tourBody: Create your first booking to start using the main Client Desk workflow.
bookingFirst.tourAction: Fill in the client details and the basic booking information here, then save the booking.

clientInfo.tourBody: Fill in the client's basic identity first in this section, such as the name and WhatsApp number.
clientInfo.tourAction: Complete the main client details here before moving on to the session details.

sessionDetails.tourBody: This section is where you set the event type, date, time, location, and main package for the booking.
sessionDetails.tourAction: Complete the main session details here so your first booking is ready to save.

submit.tourBody: Once the main details are filled in, save this booking to complete the onboarding step.
submit.tourAction: Use this save button to create your first booking and move on to the next step.

bookingsOverview.label: Review Bookings Page
bookingsOverview.description: See the booking you just saved and get familiar with the booking list and its main actions.
bookingsOverview.tourTitle: See your first booking in the list
bookingsOverview.tourBody: This page shows the bookings that have been saved, including the first booking you just created.
bookingsOverview.tourAction: Use this area to get familiar with the booking list, search, and the main actions for each booking.

clientStatus.label: Review Status Booking Page
clientStatus.description: Once you have a booking, monitor booking progress and use the tracking link from the status page.
clientStatus.tourBody: This page is where you monitor booking progress, queue status, and the tracking link you can share with clients.
clientStatus.tourAction: Use this table or these cards to review booking status and access the client tracking link.

googleCalendar.label: Connect Google Calendar
googleCalendar.description: Sync booking schedules to Google Calendar so your working agenda stays organized.
googleCalendar.tourBody: Connect your Google account if you want calendar sync to run automatically.
googleCalendar.tourAction: Use this card to connect Google Calendar from the Settings page.

googleDrive.label: Connect Google Drive
googleDrive.description: Connect Google Drive for files, QRIS assets, and automatic work folders.
googleDrive.tourBody: Connect your Google account if you want files and work folders to run automatically.
googleDrive.tourAction: Use this card to connect Google Drive from the Settings page.

Tutorial.title: Learn Client Desk in more depth without repeating the basic setup
Tutorial.description: This page helps you understand feature purpose, workflow, and best practices after your basic setup is done or already underway.
Tutorial.antiDuplication: Tutorial focuses on context, when to use each feature, and the best workflow. Setup Guide remains the place for step-by-step interactive directions.
Tutorial.topicsDescription: Start with the core workflow, then go deeper into the features you will use most often day to day.
Tutorial.stepsTitle: How to use it / click-by-click flow
Tutorial.stepsDescription: Follow this sequence when you want to try the feature directly inside the app.
Tutorial.notesDescription: These points help you use the feature more cleanly without repeating the interactive onboarding flow.

surfaces.setupDescription: Best for new users who want interactive guidance through the setup steps inside the app.
surfaces.tutorialDescription: Best for users who want to understand feature purpose, compare workflows, and learn best practices without a spotlight tour.

overview.title: Getting to know Client Desk
overview.description: Understand the main menu structure, the core work areas, and how each page supports your daily operations.

details.understand: Client Desk is split into setup, booking operations, status monitoring, and finance. Understanding what each page is for makes it much easier to know where to start when new work comes in.
details.whenToUse: This topic is most useful when you are just starting with the app, onboarding a new admin, or trying to make your team's daily workflow more consistent.
details.quickTips: Start from Dashboard for the overview, use Bookings for daily operations, use Booking Status for client-facing progress, and use Tutorial when you want context before changing your workflow.

step1.title: Open Dashboard as your operational starting point
step1.description: Use Dashboard to see booking summaries, upcoming work, and quick access to the most important pages.
step1.result: You immediately understand today's studio condition without opening every menu one by one.

step2.title: Learn the main menu in the sidebar
step2.description: Review pages like Bookings, Services / Packages, Booking Form, Team / Freelance, Finance, and Booking Status.
step2.result: You can distinguish which pages are for setup and which pages are for day-to-day operations.

step3.title: Open one booking from the Bookings page
step3.description: Go to Bookings and open one entry to see how client details, session data, and payments are structured together.
step3.result: You understand that booking detail is the central record for one client.

step4.title: Use Tutorial when you need context, not just setup
step4.description: If you are unsure what a page is for, open Tutorial. If you only want to complete setup tasks, open Setup Guide.
step4.result: You know when to use the interactive guide and when to read the deeper documentation.

notes.note1: If a new admin is joining the team, this is a good first topic before moving to more specific tutorials.
notes.note2: This tutorial explains the product flow, but it still works best when paired with direct hands-on use of the pages you open every day.

workflow.title: How the booking workflow runs from start to finish
workflow.description: Learn the overall flow from incoming leads, booking creation, session execution, all the way to status and payment follow-up.

details.understand: The main Client Desk flow usually starts from a booking form or manual booking entry, then continues into session setup, team assignment, progress tracking, and payment completion. Each page supports one part of that journey.
details.whenToUse: Use this topic when you want to make sure your internal process matches the way the app is designed to be used, or when you want to reduce repeated manual steps.
details.quickTips: Pick one consistent main workflow for all bookings. The more uniform your process is from start to finish, the easier it is for the team to read statuses and avoid scattered data.

step1.title: Decide how bookings enter the system first
step1.description: Choose whether bookings should come from the public Booking Form or be created manually by the admin from the dashboard.
step1.result: The team has a clear intake path before client data moves further.

step2.title: Complete booking and session information
step2.description: Fill in client identity, event type, schedule, location, and main package so the booking is operationally ready.
step2.result: The booking already has enough core information to be monitored and worked on.

step3.title: Monitor the booking from Bookings and Booking Status
step3.description: Use Bookings for internal admin operations and Booking Status for progress visibility and client tracking.
step3.result: You separate internal workflow needs from client-facing progress monitoring.

step4.title: Follow up payments and closing work
step4.description: As the booking progresses, use finance and status pages to keep deposits, settlement, and progress aligned.
step4.result: The full flow from intake to completion can be monitored end to end in one system.

notes.note1: The more consistent your intake path and data entry habits are, the easier it becomes for admins to search and update bookings.
notes.note2: If your internal studio process changes, revisit this topic to make sure the workflow still fits the way Client Desk is structured.

formVsManual.title: When to use Booking Form vs manual booking
formVsManual.description: See when it is more efficient to let clients submit their own form and when the admin should create the booking manually.

details.understand: Booking Form works best for direct incoming leads, while manual booking is better when data comes from chat, phone calls, referrals, or offline events that the admin needs to record manually.
details.whenToUse: Use the public form when you want cleaner self-service input from clients. Use manual booking when the admin needs full control from the beginning or when the lead did not come through the form.
details.quickTips: Choose one primary path for your studio. Many teams use the public form for new leads and reserve manual booking only for exceptions so the data stays more consistent.

step1.title: Open Booking Form to check whether the public flow is ready
step1.description: Review the form link, payment methods, and basic form setup if you want clients to submit their own booking details.
step1.result: You know whether the public form is ready to share or still needs adjustments.

step2.title: Use manual booking when the admin owns the intake
step2.description: Go to booking creation when the lead comes from chat, phone, or referrals and needs to be entered by the admin.
step2.result: Client data still gets recorded inside the system even if it did not come through the public form.

step3.title: Compare control needs against efficiency
step3.description: Share the Booking Form when you want a cleaner and more repeatable intake flow. Use manual booking when the admin must control details from the start.
step3.result: The team can choose the right intake path based on the real context, not a one-size-fits-all rule.

step4.title: Set one consistent team habit
step4.description: Align the team on when to send the form and when to create bookings manually so data does not stay scattered outside the app.
step4.result: Your intake flow becomes easier to follow and more consistent across admins.

notes.note1: If your studio often receives leads through WhatsApp, manual booking will usually remain useful even when the public form is active.
notes.note2: Booking Form and manual booking are not competing features. They are two intake paths you can use for different situations.

servicesPackages.title: How to structure services, main packages, and add-ons
servicesPackages.description: Learn how to build a clear service catalog so clients can easily understand package choices, pricing, and add-ons.

details.understand: Main packages are the core choices clients compare first, while add-ons are supporting extras. A clear structure helps both the booking form and the admin team understand client needs faster.
details.whenToUse: This topic matters when you are building your catalog for the first time, adding new offers, or noticing that clients often get confused by your package options.
details.quickTips: Keep main packages simple and easy to compare, then place extra options as add-ons. Avoid too many similar packages because they make both the client journey and admin input more confusing.

step1.title: Open Services / Packages
step1.description: Go to the Services / Packages page to review all active main packages and add-ons in your account.
step1.result: You get one clear place to structure your studio catalog.

step2.title: Click add to create a main package first
step2.description: Start with one core service that has a clear name, price, duration, and description.
step2.result: Clients have an easy main option to compare and select.

step3.title: Use add-ons only for supporting extras
step3.description: Keep optional extras separate instead of turning everything into a standalone main package.
step3.result: Your offer structure stays cleaner for both clients and admins.

step4.title: Review the result from the booking form perspective
step4.description: After saving services, open Booking Form or preview the flow to check if package order and choices still feel easy to understand.
step4.result: The catalog works not only in admin view, but also from the client's point of view.

notes.note1: If clients often struggle to choose, the issue is usually not just the number of packages but how unclear the differences feel.
notes.note2: Main packages should carry the main decision, while add-ons help refine the offer without making the catalog feel noisy.

teamAssignment.title: How to manage team/freelance and assignments
teamAssignment.description: Understand when to add team members, how assignment data is used, and why team data matters for smoother operations.

details.understand: Team and freelance data acts as the foundation for assignments, collaboration, and clarity on who is involved in each booking. It reduces dependence on scattered chat notes.
details.whenToUse: This topic becomes important when your studio starts involving editors, photographers, videographers, or freelance partners who should be tracked more systematically.
details.quickTips: Add the members who are genuinely part of regular operations first. Once the structure is clear, build the habit of reading assignments from the app instead of relying on fragmented chat threads.

step1.title: Open Team / Freelance
step1.description: Visit the Team / Freelance page to see which members are already available for collaborative work.
step1.result: You can compare the team structure in the app with your real studio workflow.

step2.title: Click add member and fill in the basics
step2.description: Enter name, role, contact details, and any practical information the team needs to recognize that member quickly.
step2.result: Your first team member or freelancer is ready to be referenced inside the app.

step3.title: Use team data once bookings become collaborative
step3.description: When a booking needs task distribution, rely on the members already recorded instead of depending only on chat threads.
step3.result: Assignments become easier to trace and manage.

step4.title: Keep the member list relevant
step4.description: Add the people who are actively used and keep the list tidy so the page stays operationally useful.
step4.result: The team page remains clean and practical instead of becoming an outdated archive.

notes.note1: You will feel the value of clean team data much more once booking volume grows and one booking involves multiple people.
notes.note2: If you do not yet manage assignments from the app, start with member records first. Assignment habits can be built gradually.

bookingList.title: How to read the Bookings page
bookingList.description: Understand the bookings list as the place to find data quickly, monitor high-level progress, and access each booking's main actions.

details.understand: The Bookings page is the operational home base for finding clients, opening booking details, checking schedules, and accessing fast actions. It is usually the page admins visit most often.
details.whenToUse: Use this topic when you want to understand how to scan all active bookings, find a specific client quickly, or move into booking details without jumping across too many screens.
details.quickTips: Treat the Bookings page as a working surface, not just a list. Use search, status cues, and fast actions to help the admin team respond faster to daily client needs.

step1.title: Open Bookings from the sidebar
step1.description: Go to the Bookings page to see all active and recent bookings in one place.
step1.result: You immediately get a high-level picture of work that is already in motion.

step2.title: Use search or visual status cues as data grows
step2.description: Search for client names or booking codes, and use visible status markers to spot the bookings that need attention.
step2.result: Admins can find relevant bookings faster instead of scanning manually.

step3.title: Open the booking you want to inspect
step3.description: Click into a booking to review client data, session details, services, payments, and related files more fully.
step3.result: You move from the operational overview into the full record for one client quickly.

step4.title: Use this page as your daily operating base
step4.description: Whenever there is a question or follow-up task, start from Bookings before moving into a more specific page.
step4.result: The team shares one consistent starting point for day-to-day admin work.

notes.note1: This page is best for quick internal operations, while Booking Status is better when you need to focus on progress and client tracking.
notes.note2: If admins often struggle to find data, the habit of using Bookings as the primary work surface is usually not yet strong enough.

bookingStatus.title: How to read the Booking Status page
bookingStatus.description: Understand the status page for managing client progress, queue order, and the tracking link you can share.

details.understand: Booking Status is focused on progress and client tracking rather than the full admin detail of a booking. It helps admins monitor work stages and gives clients better visibility through a tracking link.
details.whenToUse: This topic matters once bookings are already in progress and you need to manage queue position, update progress, or reduce repeated status questions from clients.
details.quickTips: Keep booking statuses accurate and updated. The more consistently the team maintains progress states, the lighter the manual follow-up burden becomes because clients can rely on the tracking link.

step1.title: Open Booking Status once work is in progress
step1.description: Visit the Booking Status page to review current progress and active queue positions across clients.
step1.result: You can monitor active work without opening every booking detail individually.

step2.title: Check status labels and queue ordering
step2.description: Look at the visible statuses and queue information to understand what stage each booking is currently in.
step2.result: Admins can quickly see which bookings need updates or should be prioritized.

step3.title: Use the tracking link when clients ask for updates
step3.description: When a client asks about progress, use the tracking link from this page so they can monitor their booking more independently.
step3.result: Repeated manual progress updates through chat can decrease.

step4.title: Update statuses regularly based on real progress
step4.description: Make it a team habit to update statuses when work actually moves stages instead of waiting until the end.
step4.result: The status page stays accurate and genuinely useful for both the team and clients.

notes.note1: If statuses are rarely updated, the tracking link still exists but becomes much less valuable because the information gets stale quickly.
notes.note2: Booking Status works best as an everyday operational habit, not only as a page you open when there is a complaint or a question.

finance.title: How to read finance, deposits, and settlement
finance.description: Learn how Client Desk surfaces revenue, deposits, settlement, and payment indicators that still need attention.

details.understand: The finance area helps you see incoming money, deposit status, remaining settlement, and overall revenue signals without relying on separate notes. It is designed around booking-related financial visibility.
details.whenToUse: Open this topic when you want admins to understand the difference between deposit, settlement, and the indicators that mean a payment still needs follow-up.
details.quickTips: Use finance as a monitoring layer, then make sure payment updates happen as soon as a transaction is verified. Faster updates make reporting and follow-up much more reliable.

step1.title: Open Finance to review payment summaries
step1.description: Use the Finance page to monitor revenue, deposits, and settlement items that still need attention.
step1.result: You get a clearer financial picture from one page.

step2.title: Separate deposits, settlement, and follow-up signals
step2.description: Identify which bookings have only deposit paid, which are close to settlement, and which still need payment follow-up.
step2.result: Admins can decide which bookings should be verified or reminded next.

step3.title: Open booking detail when the numbers need context
step3.description: If a payment figure or label needs verification, move into the relevant booking detail for the full transaction context.
step3.result: Finance data stays grounded in the underlying booking information.

step4.title: Update payments as soon as transactions are confirmed
step4.description: Do not delay deposit or settlement updates if the payment is already verified.
step4.result: Your finance monitoring stays much more accurate day to day.

notes.note1: The faster payments are verified and recorded, the lower the chance that admins will misread the real financial state during busy periods.
notes.note2: The finance page is best treated as an operational monitoring layer, not as a full accounting replacement.

googleIntegration.title: Google Calendar and Google Drive integrations
googleIntegration.description: Understand when Google integrations are useful and the practical value for syncing schedules, files, and collaborative work.

details.understand: Google integrations add automation, especially for schedules and files. They are not required to use Client Desk, but they become very helpful once the workload increases and collaboration gets more complex.
details.whenToUse: Consider these integrations when your studio starts coordinating more team schedules, needs more structured file handling, or wants to reduce repeated copy-paste into external tools.
details.quickTips: Finish your core setup first before connecting Google. Once the basic workflow is stable, use integrations to save time and keep data more consistent across tools.

step1.title: Open Settings after the core setup is stable
step1.description: Go to Settings to review the available Google Calendar and Google Drive integrations.
step1.result: You can judge whether your studio is ready for extra automation.

step2.title: Connect Google Calendar if scheduling needs syncing
step2.description: Enable calendar connection once the team needs more centralized and consistent schedule visibility.
step2.result: Work agendas become easier to read across tools without manual copying.

step3.title: Connect Google Drive if file handling is getting complex
step3.description: Use Drive integration when the studio needs more structured folders and more automated file handling.
step3.result: Working files become easier to organize and trace.

step4.title: Evaluate the integration after a few real bookings
step4.description: Review whether the integration actually saves time and reduces repeated manual work.
step4.result: You can confirm that the integration is useful in practice, not just enabled.

notes.note1: Google integrations are optional extras. The studio can still run the core Client Desk workflow without turning them on immediately.
notes.note2: The clearer your operational needs are, the easier it is to decide whether Calendar, Drive, or both are really necessary.

dailyTips.title: Daily usage tips and FAQ
dailyTips.description: A practical wrap-up of habits that keep your dashboard tidy, your data organized, and common questions easy to answer.

details.understand: Daily tips help the team use Client Desk consistently, not just understand features in theory. Small habits like updating status quickly or entering complete data early have a big impact on operational clarity.
details.whenToUse: This topic is useful after the basic setup is done and the team starts using the app every day. It also helps when the workflow begins to feel messy again.
details.quickTips: Use Tutorial and FAQ as references for repeated questions, then reinforce simple team habits: clean input, timely updates, and using the right page for the right kind of work.

step1.title: Start the day from Dashboard or Bookings
step1.description: Open a high-level overview first before diving into detail so the team knows what deserves attention today.
step1.result: Your workday starts from a clear picture instead of random reactions to incoming chat.

step2.title: Enter complete data as early as possible
step2.description: When creating or updating a booking, try to capture the core information up front instead of postponing it.
step2.result: Bookings are easier to process and the next admin does not have to guess missing details.

step3.title: Update status and payments as soon as they change
step3.description: Do not wait too long to update booking progress or payment states once the real change has happened.
step3.result: Operational pages stay accurate enough for the rest of the team to trust.

step4.title: Use Tutorial and FAQ as shared references
step4.description: When the same questions appear repeatedly, point the team to Tutorial or FAQ instead of rewriting the same explanations in chat.
step4.result: Operational knowledge becomes easier to share and maintain.

notes.note1: Clean operations are usually shaped less by advanced features and more by small habits that stay consistent every day.
notes.note2: This tutorial becomes much more useful when the team treats it as a shared reference instead of something that is read once and forgotten.
